DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT GMRI, INC. DBA OLIVE GARDEN ITALIAN RESTAURANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER CODE COMPLINANT RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 57, 58 AND 59, PURSUANT TO CCP 2030.220. PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO MAKE A GOOD FAITH EFFORT AND REASONABLE INQUIRY. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 61. DEFENDANT HAS A LESS INTRUSTIVE MEANS AVAILABLE OF OBTAINING RELEVENT MEDICAL AND BILLING RECORDS. CCP 2017.020(A). DEFENDANT'S MOTION AS TO INTERROGATORIES 62-64 IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF IS CLAIMING THE SAME OR SIMILIAR INJURIES IN THE SUBJECT ACTION. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER FURTHER REGARDING THE SCOPE OF THE RECORDS SOUGHT AND REDACTION OF THE RECORDS. A PROPOSED ORDER HAS BEEN SUBMITTED AND BEING REVIEWED BY THE COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GMRI, INC. DBA OLIVE GARDEN ITALIAN RESTAURANT's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions.

Defendant's motion is granted as follows: Plaintiff shall provide further code complinant responses to Special Interrogatories 57, 58 and 59, pursuant to CCP 2030.220. Plaintiff is required to make a good faith effort and reasonable inquiry. The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory 61. Defendant has a less intrustive means available of obtaining relevent medical and billing records. CCP 2017.020(a). Defendant's motion as to Interrogatories 62-64 is granted to the extent that Plaintiff is claiming the same or similiar injuries in the subject action. The parties shall meet and confer further regarding the scope of the records sought and redaction of the records.

A Proposed order has been submitted and being reviewed by the Court.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
